Numerical phase + ratio compensation for Dyn11: 30° lag LV behind HV, ratio 200:4000 normalised in relay

protection_settings.set_unit: × In bias slope (30% slope 1), pickup 20% In = 26 A primary HV-side equivalent
protection_settings.ct_ratio: 200/5 HV + 4000/5 LV (numerical ratio compensation in relay)

protection_settings.set_unit: A primary (1.2× HV FLA 131 A, 1.83× CT primary 200 A)

assumptions: ABB RET615 or equivalent numerical biased-differential relay assumed for R87T — modern numerical relays handle the Dyn11 phase shift (30° lag LV behind HV) and the CT ratio mismatch (200:4000) internally in firmware, eliminating the need for interposing CTs.

CT class 5P20 selected for both HV and LV CTs per IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2: 20× accuracy limit factor accommodates the worst-case through-fault current 35 kA LV / 18 kA HV reflected through the CT — saturation knee held above the maximum through-fault to preserve differential stability.

Bias slope 30% (slope 1) and pickup 20% In are the standard ABB / Siemens recommended starting settings for distribution-transformer differential per the manufacturer commissioning guides; second-harmonic blocking (15% I2/I1) and fifth-harmonic blocking (35% I5/I1) assumed enabled in the relay to prevent magnetising-inrush and over-excitation false trips.

Backup R51 IDMT phase overcurrent set at 240 A primary (1.2× HV FLA, 1.83× CT primary) on VI curve TMS 0.3 — operates ~1.2 s at 6 kA HV fault, grades above the 87T instantaneous trip and below the upstream 11 kV ring-main relay.

fault-level intent consumed: ifault_ka_HV = 18 kA, ifault_ka_LV = 35 kA. Both values confirm CT class 5P20 (saturation knee held above through-fault) and the bias-slope 30% selection (stable against through-fault CT mismatch errors up to 5%).

db-layout-rollup intent consumed: LV main switch fed_from = T1 secondary, main_switch_rating_a = 4000 A. The LV ACB Q1-LV rating matches the consumed cascade. ENA G99 grid code (UK DNO requirement for embedded generation interface — not directly applicable to this passive consumer transformer but cited as the framework reference for HV-side protection settings approval by the DNO).

rationale.chat_summary: 2.5 MVA 11kV/0.4kV transformer differential protection schematic for a UK commercial intake (Dyn11). R87T biased differential (30% slope, 20% In pickup) with numerical phase + ratio compensation for the Dyn11 vector, plus R51 HV backup (VI curve TMS 0.3, 1.2× FLA pickup). Two lockouts K86-HV + K86-LV trip both ends for full transformer isolation. fault-level + db-layout-rollup intents both consumed. Reply to refine.

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: protection_differential on sheet PROT-002 for a UK commercial intake 2.5 MVA Dyn11 11kV/0.4kV transformer. Two intents consumed: fault-level (ifault_ka_HV = 18 kA, ifault_ka_LV = 35 kA) and db-layout-rollup (LV main switch fed_from = transformer secondary, main_switch_rating_a = 4000 A). GB jurisdiction applies BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 314 (compatibility and grouping) for the transformer-zone selectivity, IEC 60255-187 for the differential relay, and ENA G99 grid code as the DNO interface framework for HV settings approval.

decisions.label: 87T as primary, 51 as backup at 2.5 MVA
decisions.summary: 2.5 MVA crosses the threshold where transformer differential is the standard primary scheme; IDMT 51 retained as backup against an 87T relay failure or DC supply loss.
decisions.rule: Transformer protection scheme selection by MVA rating
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60255-187 (biased differential relay) + IEEE C37.91 (transformer protection guide, cross-cited for UK industrial practice)

decisions.label: Two-lockout architecture: K86-HV + K86-LV
decisions.summary: Separate lockouts on both ends ensure that a 87T or 51 trip clears the transformer from BOTH sources — preventing back-feed from the LV side through a closed LV ACB onto a faulted transformer.

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: 11 kV three-phase HV incomer from DNO ring-main unit feeding 2.5 MVA Dyn11 transformer; LV secondary 0.4 kV TN-S supplying a commercial intake LV main switch (4000 A ACB). HV FLA = 2500 / (√3 × 11) = 131 A. LV FLA = 2500 / (√3 × 0.4) = 3608 A. CT primary HV 200/5 sized at 1.5× HV FLA; CT primary LV 4000/5 sized at 1.1× LV FLA (deliberately close to LV FLA because the LV CTs are typically integrated with the ACB current sensors and have less ratio flexibility).

decisions.label: HV CT primary 200 A at 1.5× FLA
decisions.summary: 1.5× pickup margin gives differential-scheme stability without unduly compressing the differential operating region.
decisions.rule: CT ratio selection for transformer differential

decisions.label: fault-level intent consumed: 18 kA HV, 35 kA LV
decisions.summary: Both fault levels used to verify CT class 5P20 saturation knee and bias-slope stability margin against through-fault CT errors.

decisions.label: db-layout-rollup intent consumed: LV ACB 4000 A
decisions.summary: main_switch_rating_a = 4000 A matches the LV CT primary 4000/5 — no rating mismatch between protection CT and load-current sensor.

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: Fifteen devices placed: 6× CTs (3 HV @ 200/5 + 3 LV @ 4000/5, all class 5P20), R87T (biased differential relay, ABB RET615 or equivalent), R51-HVBU (HV-side backup IDMT, ABB REF615 or equivalent), Q1-HV (11 kV CB) + Q1-LV (4000 A LV ACB), K86-HV + K86-LV (separate hand-reset lockouts per end), plus three terminal blocks X1 (HV CT secondary shorting), X2 (LV CT secondary shorting), X3 (110 VDC trip circuits). The 87T relay handles Dyn11 phase shift and CT ratio mismatch numerically — no interposing CTs required, which is the modern practice for digital relays.

decisions.label: CT class 5P20 for both HV + LV (saturation knee at 20×In)
decisions.summary: 5P20 ensures CT linearity up to 20× rated current — saturation knee at 4000 A HV-side secondary (20 × 200) and 80 kA LV-side secondary (20 × 4000), both above the consumed fault levels (18 kA HV / 35 kA LV).
decisions.rule: CT class selection for biased differential

decisions.label: Numerical Dyn11 vector + ratio compensation (no interposing CTs)
decisions.summary: Modern numerical relays (RET615, 7UT63, P64x) handle the Dyn11 30° phase shift and the 200:4000 ratio mismatch internally — no auxiliary interposing CTs needed, simpler wiring and lower commissioning risk.

sections.title: Protection Coordination
sections.summary: R87T biased differential: bias slope 30% (slope 1), pickup 20% In = 26 A primary HV-equivalent. Instantaneous unrestrained pickup at 800% In for high-current internal faults. Second-harmonic blocking 15% I2/I1 prevents magnetising-inrush false trip; fifth-harmonic blocking 35% I5/I1 prevents over-excitation false trip. R51-HVBU backup: pickup 240 A primary (1.2× HV FLA), VI curve TMS 0.3 — operates ~1.2 s at 6 kA fault, grading above 87T (instantaneous) and below the upstream DNO 11 kV ring-main relay (~2 s at 6 kA per ENA G99 typical settings).

decisions.label: Bias slope 30%, pickup 20% In
decisions.summary: Standard ABB / Siemens / Schneider recommended starting settings for distribution transformers — gives stability against through-fault CT-error currents up to ~5% mismatch.

decisions.label: Backup R51 VI curve TMS 0.3
decisions.summary: Very Inverse curve at TMS 0.3 — pickup 240 A primary (1.83× CT primary), operates ~1.2 s at 6 kA. Grades above 87T (instantaneous) and below the DNO ring-main relay.
decisions.rule: Backup protection grading per ENA G99
decisions.code_clause: ENA Engineering Recommendation G99 Issue 1 (UK DNO grid-code framework) + IEC 60255-151

decisions.label: Two-end trip on K86-HV + K86-LV
decisions.summary: 87T trip energises BOTH lockouts via parallel trip outputs — Q1-HV opens AND Q1-LV opens; no single-end isolation gap.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 314 (selectivity grouping) + § 537.2 (isolation), IEC 60255-187 (biased differential relays), IEC 60255-151 (overcurrent backup relay), IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 (CT performance — IEC 60044-1 still commonly cited despite supersession), ENA G99 (UK DNO grid code interface framework), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (graphical symbols). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Six assumptions documented including the numerical relay choice, CT class rationale, harmonic-blocking enablement, backup-relay grading, and both consumed-intent declarations.

decisions.label: GB jurisdiction citation discipline
decisions.summary: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 314 / § 537.2 cited directly as the operative UK installation standard; IEC 60255 / IEC 60044 / ENA G99 cited as jurisdiction-agnostic protection / grid-code standards.

decisions.label: ENA G99 cited as DNO interface framework
decisions.summary: Although ENA G99 primarily covers embedded-generation interface requirements, it is the UK framework reference for HV-side relay settings approval by the DNO — cited as the framework even on a passive consumer transformer.

sections.title: Cross-Skill Cascade Verification
sections.summary: fault-level intent supplied ifault_ka_HV = 18 kA + ifault_ka_LV = 35 kA — both values cross-checked against CT class 5P20 (saturation knee held above max through-fault) and against the bias slope (30% slope tolerates through-fault CT-error currents up to ~5% mismatch, well above the worst-case ratio-error contribution from the 200:4000 transformer ratio normalisation). db-layout-rollup intent supplied main_switch_rating_a = 4000 A on the LV side, which matches the LV CT primary 4000/5 — no mismatch between protection sensor and load-current sensor.

decisions.label: fault-level intent cross-check passed
decisions.summary: 5P20 saturation knee at 20× CT primary = 4000 A HV / 80 kA LV — both above the consumed fault levels.

decisions.label: db-layout-rollup intent cross-check passed
decisions.summary: LV CT primary 4000/5 matches LV main_switch_rating_a from the consumed intent — sensor / load-current alignment.
